source.notes:
Summary:
- DoD-facing contact center operator supporting critical comms (emergency dispatch, infra ops).
- Must deploy in completely air-gapped on-prem enclave maintained by customer SOC. No egress to public internet allowed; outbound updates require packaged media and manual approval.
- Primary features wanted: agent assist (real-time suggestions + canned responses), ticket summarization (post-call TL;DR), tool-calling integration (RA/TA to call internal CRM/dispatch tools), offline reranking for search across document stores, and automated playbook triggers for high-severity incidents.

Key constraints/requirements:
- Security: FIPS 140-2 HSM required for key management, SOC2+ISO27001 posture, detailed audit logs retained 7+ years, SIEM integration (Splunk), SSO via SAML and RBAC.
- Compliance: anticipating IL4/DoD STIG checks; vendor to provide SBOM, signed supply chain attestations.
- Network: air-gapped; deployment needs OOB update process. No external telemetry.
- Performance: support ~1,500 concurrent agents with peak bursts to 4,500. Target 95th pctile suggestion latency < 200ms for short responses (agent-assist), overall tail latency requirement conservative.
- Model footprint: prefer quantized 8-bit or 4-bit variants; ability to run on isolated GPU clusters (customer standard is NVIDIA A40 fleet).

Recent timeline (bullets):
- 2026-03-03: Intro call (Jordan + Priya) with ops lead; initial use-case brief (agent assist + ticket summarization). (fireflies:call-20260311-ihco-01)
- 2026-03-11: Security deep-dive; SIEM/KMS expectations documented; customer requested SBOM and HSM integration notes. (gmail thread:1782a1b4_ihco_security_qna)
- 2026-03-18: Architecture review w/ infra team — reviewed network diagram and constraints. Action: produce air-gapped deployment runbook. (drive:1ihco-poc-runbook)
- 2026-03-24: Demo of on-prem agent-assist prototype in isolated lab; customer requested throughput testing scenario. (fireflies:call-20260324-ihco-02)
- 2026-03-26: Security team submitted procurement checklist; awaiting vendor attestation (ticket SEC-422).

POC scope and success criteria:
- Full air-gapped install of Redwood Private in customer enclave (no egress) using provided install media.
- Integrate with on-prem Genesys/Avaya connectors (tool-calling) and Splunk for audit logs.
- Latency: 95th percentile suggestion < 200ms under 1,500 concurrent agents; scale test to bursts of 4,500.
- Summaries: automated ticket summarization accuracy baseline (human eval) >= 85% on selected dataset.
- Operational: demonstrate signed update workflow, SBOM, and HSM-backed key rotation.

Quotes and call snippets:
- "We cannot have any outbound telemetry — this is a hard line." — Ops director
- "If you can show a clean air-gapped install with HSM keys, procurement will move fast." — Security lead

Open items and blockers (short):
- Need Redwood to produce: SBOM, FIPS/HSM integration docs, signed supply chain attestation, IL4-ready checklist.
- Procurement timeline uncertain: internal funding cycle closes mid-May.
- On-prem install window: customer proposed week of 2026-05-11 for POC run.

Next steps (actionable):
- Jordan: send updated SOW and NDA for signature (in progress).
- Priya: prepare air-gapped deployment runbook and HSM integration notes (deliver by 2026-04-05).
- Schedule: lock POC week after SOW signed; request customer confirm A40 node counts and storage IO targets.

Attachments and refs: security deck (drive), poc-runbook (drive), procurement checklist (attached ticket SEC-422).

Notes style: short, fragmented, CRM-friendly. Record reflects multiple parties: AE (Jordan), SE (Priya), CSM (Liam).

source.notes:
Status: mid-market product org evaluating Redwood. Short background: Bridgepoint builds a personalization and support layer that uses embeddings + reranking to power the in-product help center and recommended content. Started on Hosted API to validate latency/cost; moving to Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation.

POC summary: 14-day hosted POC completed. Key metrics: avg token cost (hosted) higher than target; latency within SLO for short chats (median 60ms) but occasional tail spikes at 95th pctl ~110-130ms. SE tuning (Priya) improved batching and cache hit rate to 42%. They want Dedicated for lower unit cost and better isolation.

Pricing/packaging discussion notes (primary focus):
- Bridgepoint wants 3-year preferred pricing but finance hesitant; leaning toward 12-24 month commit.
- Primary ask: commit-level to cover baseline 50qps equivalent (est. sustained throughput for peak page views).
- Burst: need guaranteed burst capacity to 150qps for 30m daily peak windows. Discussed burst as reserved credits vs. auto-burst with capped overage.
- Overage model: customer pushes for flat $/1k tokens overage with a negotiated floor; finance asked for tiered overage with tightening at high usage.
- Ramp schedule: they request a 60-day ramp (start at 40% commit, step to 70% at day30, 100% at day60) to align with release calendar.
- Pricing levers: suggested offer includes 12-mo commit with 10% discount vs. pay-as-you-go, burst pool + monthly invoice for burst, and 90-day price protection for model upgrades.

Security/compliance: SSO/SAML required, audit logs forwarded to their SIEM, KMS integration mandatory for encryption-at-rest. Also ask for data residency for EU customers. SOC2 report requested; legal has to sign DSAs.

Procurement notes: Bridgepoint procurement needs line-item pricing for commit + burst + overage; also wants explicit SLA for Dedicated latency targets (95th pctl < 150ms). Finance flagged internal approval needed for any >$150k ARR commit.

Quotes / direct language from call: "We need to be sure that bursts won't tank our costs — we need predictability for Q3 launch." — VP Product.

Next actions: Jordan to deliver final SOW v1 with 3 commit options (12mo/24mo/36mo) and burst terms by 2026-03-18; Priya to validate KV cache improvements on Dedicated test harness; Legal to begin DPA/DSA review once SOW shared.

Notes artifacts: pricing-proposal-v2.pdf (drive), security-faq.pdf (drive), Fireflies ff_2026-02-22-bridgepoint-demo.

Decided to evaluate Dedicated for predictable unit economics and SLA.
pricing_terms_discussed: 3 tier commit options: 50K tokens/day, 150K tokens/day, 500K tokens/day (monthly commit)
Burst bucket: 30% of commit available for short bursts with 5x overage rate cap
Overage pricing: 1.5x unit rate for up to 20% monthly overage; additional overage billed at 2.5x with credit threshold
Ramp schedule proposal: 3-month ramp (50% month1, 75% month2, 100% month3) with prorated billing and no overage charges during ramp windows
Minimum monthly commitment discount: 12% off hosted list for mid-tier commit; enterprise tier custom pricing

Procurement asked for a 60-day trial clause for Dedicated or conversion credit if performance/latency targets not met. Discussed pilot invoice structure vs prepay reserve. AE proposed staggered payments tied to ramp milestones.
source.technical_requirements: VPC peering + private IP routes, SSO (SAML) integration, KMS-managed keys for model request logs, 12-month retention with purge flags, regional residency (us-east-1 preferred). Latency target: P95 <100ms for chat; throughput ~120 QPS for embeddings during peak.
source.negotiation_playbook_notes: Offer 6-month pilot commit at mid-tier with a defined ramp and burst allowances; include 30-day performance SLA and crediting formula; lock price for 12 months; include opt-out clause if SOC2 + DPA not accepted within 45 days.

Head of ML (J. Kim): 'Dedicated gives us confidence on cache behavior and predictable latency; want a clear plan for model upgrades/fallbacks.'

Phase 2 (dedicated pilot) - 6 weeks: deploy mid-tier Dedicated pool, validate P95 latency, run canary model swap
Phase 3 (scale) - 3 months post-pilot: ramp to committed level per schedule, integrate billing/alerting
notes: Primary drivers: cost predictability, SLA, VPC/private network for customer data flow.
POC uncovered underestimation of token churn on long conversations; recommending intelligent prefix caching + short-term KV retention.
They asked for a mapped example invoice showing commit, burst, and overage across a 90-day ramp — sales ops to generate.
Legal: want Redwood DPA + custom SLA clause for 99.9% inference availability; indemnity language flagged by their GC.

Good signal: product team already built integration with Hosted API; engineering + infra are enthusiastic about Dedicated control plane.

source.notes:
Account background: mid-tier EU payments platform expanding into regulated lending. Primary concern = data residency and custodial separation. POC scope: private VPC deployment in eu-west-1, BYOK KMS integration, audit log shipping to customer SIEM, region locks for production.

Timeline (high-level):
- 2026-01-15: Intro call (Jordan + Priya) — overview of Redwood capabilities; customer asked for EU-only production.
- 2026-01-28: Sent security questionnaire (completed v1).
- 2026-02-05: Architecture review with infra team — agreed on VPC peering model and egress controls. Action: provide network diagram + CIDR ranges.
- 2026-02-22: Security deep-dive recorded (fireflies ff_2026-02-22_9a7b2c). Discussed KMS trust model, HSM attestation, and audit log schema. Quote from CISO: "production data must never leave EU borders without documented DTIA + SCCs".
- 2026-03-03: POC kickoff — test workloads (chat + rerank), target throughput 500 req/s under 3 models, latency goal: p95 < 300ms for short-context queries.
- 2026-03-10: KMS demo (Priya) — customer requested FIPS 140-2 HSM proof and ability to rotate keys from their KMS.
- 2026-03-12: Legal escalated — require DPA + UK Addendum + Canada residency options for roadmap.

Requirements summary:
- Data residency: EU-only for production (mandatory). Roadmap: UK and Canada regional controls for Q4 2026.
- KMS: BYOK required; HSM attestation and ability to import/export under strict policy.
- Audit & Monitoring: Real-time audit logs to customer SIEM (splunk), immutable storage for 7 years, syslog + S3 export.
- Identity & Access: SSO (SAML) + SCIM for provisioning; role-based access controls and token TTL limits.
- Compliance docs: SOC2 + ISO27001 copies requested (provided), need SCCs and UK Addendum examples in DPA.

POC success criteria (customer):
- Demonstrate KMS integration and key rotation without downtime.
- Confirm audit log delivery and retention semantics.
- Show region lock (no data replication outside EU) during test and describe fallbacks.
- Validate latency SLOs for eu-west-1.

Risks & notes:
- Legal requires explicit contractual language for EU-only residency — potential procurement delay.
- Cost sensitivity moderate — wants cost/perf tradeoffs (quantization + batching) from Redwood Optimize.
- Expansion plan: after EU launch, customer will evaluate UK and Canada regions; asked for timeline and gating criteria.

Current next actions:
- Provide DPA + SCCs + UK Addendum template (legal).
- Deliver KMS/HSM attestation artifacts and design doc by 2026-03-19.
- Network diagram + CIDR ranges to be confirmed by customer network team (due 2026-03-18).

Stubs/links: Gmail thread 'thread-gm-AX72k2_europa-poc', drive deck 'europa-shield-private-poc-2026.pdf', security FAQ link above.

AE notes: Customer is pragmatic, heavy on legal — prioritize DPA and KMS artifacts to keep momentum. SE has good rapport; CISO is risk-averse but open to Redwood Optimize cost mitigations.

Account background: mid-tier EU payments platform expanding into regulated lending. Primary concern = data residency and custodial separation. POC scope: private VPC deployment in eu-west-1, BYOK KMS integration, audit log shipping to customer SIEM, region locks for production.

Timeline (high-level):
- 2026-01-15: Intro call (Jordan + Priya) — overview of Redwood capabilities; customer asked for EU-only production.
- 2026-01-28: Sent security questionnaire (completed v1).
- 2026-02-05: Architecture review with infra team — agreed on VPC peering model and egress controls. Action: provide network diagram + CIDR ranges.
- 2026-02-22: Security deep-dive recorded (fireflies ff_2026-02-22_9a7b2c). Discussed KMS trust model, HSM attestation, and audit log schema. Quote from CISO: "production data must never leave EU borders without documented DTIA + SCCs".
- 2026-03-03: POC kickoff — test workloads (chat + rerank), target throughput 500 req/s under 3 models, latency goal: p95 < 300ms for short-context queries.
- 2026-03-10: KMS demo (Priya) — customer requested FIPS 140-2 HSM proof and ability to rotate keys from their KMS.
- 2026-03-12: Legal escalated — require DPA + UK Addendum + Canada residency options for roadmap.

Requirements summary:
- Data residency: EU-only for production (mandatory). Roadmap: UK and Canada regional controls for Q4 2026.
- KMS: BYOK required; HSM attestation and ability to import/export under strict policy.
- Audit & Monitoring: Real-time audit logs to customer SIEM (splunk), immutable storage for 7 years, syslog + S3 export.
- Identity & Access: SSO (SAML) + SCIM for provisioning; role-based access controls and token TTL limits.
- Compliance docs: SOC2 + ISO27001 copies requested (provided), need SCCs and UK Addendum examples in DPA.

POC success criteria (customer):
- Demonstrate KMS integration and key rotation without downtime.
- Confirm audit log delivery and retention semantics.
- Show region lock (no data replication outside EU) during test and describe fallbacks.
- Validate latency SLOs for eu-west-1.

Risks & notes:
- Legal requires explicit contractual language for EU-only residency — potential procurement delay.
- Cost sensitivity moderate — wants cost/perf tradeoffs (quantization + batching) from Redwood Optimize.
- Expansion plan: after EU launch, customer will evaluate UK and Canada regions; asked for timeline and gating criteria.

Current next actions:
- Provide DPA + SCCs + UK Addendum template (legal).
- Deliver KMS/HSM attestation artifacts and design doc by 2026-03-19.
- Network diagram + CIDR ranges to be confirmed by customer network team (due 2026-03-18).

Stubs/links: Gmail thread 'thread-gm-AX72k2_europa-poc', drive deck 'europa-shield-private-poc-2026.pdf', security FAQ link above.

source.notes:
Account summary:
- Polaris runs a B2B support outsourcing platform used by EU retail and fintech customers. High SLAs for agent UX (fast suggestions) and strict data residency for EU tenants.
- Primary objective: deploy RAG-backed agent-assist + self-serve deflection widget that returns precise top-5 docs for agents and an auto-suggest short answer for end-users.

Scale + infra expectations:
- Corpus estimate: 8M historical tickets + 16M chat snippets -> ~24M passages after chunking. Expected vector count: ~24M (1536-d). Estimated index footprint: ~2.5-3.5 TB depending on index format + quantization.
- Query profile: baseline 120 QPS, peak bursts to 500 QPS for campaign days; traffic is highly diurnal and tenant-segmented.
- Latency SLO: 95th percentile end-to-end search + rerank <= 180ms (strict internal requirement). Must have documented fallback behavior at >200ms.
- Relevance target: top-5 precision >= 90% on Polaris internal dev set. Business: agents will ignore suggestions below this threshold.
- Retrieval design: ANN retrieval for top500 candidates -> rerank with cross-encoder (quantized) to top5. Also evaluate tuned bi-encoder + inexpensive pairwise scorer for cost trade-offs.
- Cost target: amortized search+rerank $/query <= $0.0025 under typical load; allow higher for dedicated private nodes during business hours.
- Multi-tenant: require per-tenant index partitioning or tenant-tagged shards and encryption-at-rest with customer KMS.

POC & recent timeline:
- 2026-01-20: AE intro (Ethan) + initial NDA.
- 2026-01-28: Scoping call (ff_2026_01_28_polar-01) 10: agreed sample export + EU residency constraints.
- 2026-02-05: Sample export (500k passages) ingested to Redwood test cluster.
- 2026-02-20: First quality run (ff_2026_02_20_polar-02) 10: top-5 precision ~87% with bi-encoder + lightweight rerank; latency 95p ~210ms with current Dedicated config.
- 2026-03-02: Engineering deep-dive with SE (Lena) 10: discussed FAISS sharding, vector compression, and HNSW parameters. Jira RWD-4399 created for sharding test.
- 2026-03-09: Internal cost model draft produced; cross-encoder at 400 QPS estimated $/query ~ $0.004 (concern). RWD-4420 opened for quantized re-ranker experiments.
- 2026-03-11: Legal submitted residency questionnaire; Polaris emphasized tenant-level EU pinning and audit log retention 7 years.

Open questions / technical risks:
- Can Redwood Dedicated + quantized kernels hit 95p <= 180ms at 400-500 QPS bursts without >2.5x baseline cost? Need guidance on batching, KV cache, and model quantization knobs.
- Operational: incremental re-embedding strategy for 24M vectors (nightly vs streaming) and impact on query availability.
- Security: tenant-level KMS integration and audit log retention impact on storage costs.

Customer quotes/highlights:
- "90% top-5 or we won't roll to agents; false positives cost trust."
- "EU customers will not accept cross-border storage for some tenants."

Next actions (immediate):
- Redwood: run Dedicated POC with FAISS sharding + quantized cross-encoder (fp16/int8) and provide per-component latency breakdown (ANN, transfer, rerank) by 2026-03-25. Include $/query at baseline and peak.
- Polaris: provide tenant list with residency requirements and a 1M passage sample for incremental reindex test.
- Legal: sync 2026-03-18 to validate residency appendix.

CRM quick notes for AE view:
- Strong procurement intent; finance prepared for enterprise commit if POC shows latency + unit economics.
- Main negotiating levers: Private EU residency and predictable per-query pricing for rerank.
- Risks: rerank cost and reindex operational complexity.

source.notes:
Summary: enterprise retail analytics vendor evaluating Redwood Dedicated for low-latency chat + reranking workloads. Procurement focused on SLA contract specifics (uptime %, credit schedules, maintenance windows, support response times).

Recent activity timeline:
- 2026-01-20: initial intro call (Liam). high-level requirements: 99.95%+ uptime target, p95 latency target 120ms for chat, 40k QPS embedding batch windows overnight.
- 2026-02-18: deep-dive w/ SE (Marisol). discussed reserved GPU planning: baseline 16xA100/80GB equivalent for peak; auto-burst to 24 on predictable seasonal spikes. SE provided throughput projection spreadsheet (see drive).
- 2026-03-02: benchmarking demo of Dedicated cluster in staging. Observed steady-state p50=85ms, p95=140ms on our test prompt mix — needs tuning. Marisol suggested prefix caching + quantization profile to hit p95 target.
- 2026-03-05: procurement + legal call. Legal flagged SLA credit schedule and definition of downtime vs. maintenance. Requested explicit maintenance windows and blackout periods.
- 2026-03-09: follow-up with solutions + infra to model reserve-sizing for 99.95 vs 99.9 uptime scenarios (Jira INF-4721).

POC acceptance criteria (customer-provided):
- Throughput: sustain 12K concurrent chat sessions at average turn length 30 tokens with <150ms p95 for user-facing routes.
- Batch/analytics: overnight embeddings throughputs >= 40K docs/hour with predictable cost curve.
- Reliability: SLA-backed availability >= 99.95% or negotiated credits that make lower uptime unacceptable.
- Security: SAML SSO + audit logging + encrypted at-rest w/ customer KMS.

SLA negotiation points (open):
- Uptime target: Archipelago pushing for 99.95% for Dedicated; Redwood initially proposed 99.9 for reserved capacity. Customer legal prefers 99.95 + financial credits.
- Credit schedule: Archipelago requested stepwise credits starting at 5% monthly credit for 0.01% below SLA; Redwood counters with a tiered schedule (0.1% increments). Legal flagged need for a maximum aggregate credit cap.
- Maintenance windows: Customer asks for pre-scheduled maintenance restricted to Sunday 02:00-06:00 local time and no more than 4 hours/month. Redwood standard M/W are twice-monthly 03:00 UTC 2 hour windows. Need to reconcile timezones and emergency patch policy.
- Support response times: Archipelago requires P1 response within 30 minutes and P1 on-call resolution SLA (escalation to engineering) within 4 hours for production-impacting incidents. Redwood standard: P1 response 1 hour, P1 resolution target 8 hours.
- Reserved GPU planning: request for explicit failure/fallback policy when reserved pool degraded (hot-standby capacity, cross-region failover). Also want cost of keeping idle reserved GPUs vs. burst model analyzed.

Notes from recent conversations / quotes:
- "We will not accept credit language that is purely future-service credits — must be bill-level credits applicable to Dedicated monthly invoice." — Archipelago procurement lead.
- "If p95 can't be guaranteed, we need clear fallback: routing to a smaller-family model with explicit latency bounds or a degraded SLA tier." — Head of infra.

Action items & next steps:
- SE (Marisol) to produce final reserved sizing runbook including cost delta for 99.9 vs 99.95 by 2026-03-17.
- Legal (Redwood) to send revised SLA draft with proposed credit cap + concrete examples of credit calculation by 2026-03-20 (drive link).
- Pricing team to provide binding quote with monthly reserved GPU count + burst pricing and amortized ARR impact for both 12-month and 36-month commit options by 2026-03-18.
- Liam to schedule final negotiation call w/ Archipelago procurement & legal 2026-03-24.

Sticking points to watch:
- Customer insistence on narrow maintenance windows + low tolerance for emergency downtime.
- Support SLAs; will likely require upgraded enterprise SLA (higher cost).
- Need explicit cross-region failover guarantees or documented recovery time objective for node failures.

Links & refs: pricing deck (drive), benchmark logs (attached to ff_20260302_z7p4), Jira INF-4721 for sizing, SEC-1102 for KMS/crypto questions.

source.notes:
Account overview: LichenRidge is a mid-market B2B SaaS analytics vendor (product analytics, in-app guidance). High growth; strong enterprise pipeline.

Engagement summary:
- Jan 15: inbound trial with Hosted API, asked for chat + embeddings. AE Marco assigned.
- Jan 28: Demo (Marco + Priya). Showed hosted streaming, function calling. Good feedback on latency.
- Feb 02: Hosted API POC started (conversation agent + semantic search). SE ran baseline perf tests (see drive link).
- Feb 11: Fireflies demo call ff_2026-02-11_lichenridge_demo_83a1 (recording). CTO: "need predictable costs for scale, can't have big spikes in month 1".
- Feb 25: POC results: average 95th perc latency 210ms for chat (prompt+context 1.2k tokens), embeddings QPS ~140, token cost projection 220k tokens/day baseline.
- Mar 03: Discussion moved to Dedicated for predictability. LichenRidge requested commit-based pricing with burst to cover product launches.
- Mar 05: Redwood provided initial Dedicated proposal: 4 reserved GPU pools, committed throughput ~300k tokens/day (equiv), monthly commit term 12 months, burst allowance up to 2x for first 3 months, overage priced at 1.5x base token rate.
- Mar 07: SE (Priya) ran VPC peering checklist with their infra: both sides ok but need IP allowlist and KMS access pattern documented.
- Mar 10: Commercial deep-dive with Marco + Head of Finance. Main negotiation points: ramp schedule (month 1 commit 50%, month 2 75%, month 3 full), burst caps during launches, overage cap protection for first 90 days, and an option to increase commit at fixed uplift after month 6. Finance wants clearer ARR waterfall and mitigation if usage is undercommit.

Technical notes / requirements:
- Must support SSO (SAML) for developer console access.
- KMS integration mandatory for key wrapping; audit logs retention 1 year.
- Data residency: EU tenant for analytics dataset, routing by region required.
- Fallback policy desired: route to smaller quantized variant if primary model capacity saturated.

Commercial points captured (open):
- Commit sizing: customer asks to set initial commit equivalent to 300k tokens/day but billable ramp months expected.
- Burst: customer requests 2.5x burst for 7-day product launches, Redwood proposed 2x for 90 days trial then review.
- Overage: they want overage softened (cap at +25% of monthly invoice for first 6 months). Legal flagged retention/residency language.

Next steps (short):
- Marco to send updated term sheet by 2026-03-15 with revised ramp and burst language.
- Priya to validate VPC peering runbook and produce KMS integration notes by 2026-03-17.
- Legal to exchange data residency clause redlines (drive link).

Sales cues and value drivers:
- Emphasize predictable unit economics and burst safety net for product launches.
- Highlight Optimize recommendations to lower token footprint (prefix caching, batching) to reduce commit needs.
- Offer a 3-month 'soft overage' buffer (account credits) as concession during onboarding.

Quotes / call snippets:
- CTO: "We can absorb some committed cost if we know bursts won't blow our budget on day 1."
- Head of Finance: "Need a clear ramp so we can forecast ARR into our revenue model."

title: LichenRidge Platforms
preview:
Account overview: LichenRidge is a mid-market B2B SaaS analytics vendor (product analytics, in-app guidance). High growth; strong enterprise pipeline.

Engagement summary:
- Jan 15: inbound trial with Hosted API, asked for chat + embeddings. AE Marco assigned.
- Jan 28: Demo (Marco + Priya). Showed hosted streaming, function calling. Good feedback on latency.
- Feb 02: Hosted API POC started (conversation agent + semantic search). SE ran baseline perf tests (see drive link).
- Feb 11: Fireflies demo call ff_2026-02-11_lichenridge_demo_83a1 (recording). CTO: "need predictable costs for scale, can't have big spikes in month 1".
- Feb 25: POC results: average 95th perc latency 210ms for chat (prompt+context 1.2k tokens), embeddings QPS ~140, token cost projection 220k tokens/day baseline.
- Mar 03: Discussion moved to Dedicated for predictability. LichenRidge requested commit-based pricing with burst to cover product launches.
- Mar 05: Redwood provided initial Dedicated proposal: 4 reserved GPU pools, committed throughput ~300k tokens/day (equiv), monthly commit term 12 months, burst allowance up to 2x for first 3 months, overage priced at 1.5x base token rate.
- Mar 07: SE (Priya) ran VPC peering checklist with their infra: both sides ok but need IP allowlist and KMS access pattern documented.
- Mar 10: Commercial deep-dive with Marco + Head of Finance. Main negotiation points: ramp schedule (month 1 commit 50%, month 2 75%, month 3 full), burst caps during launches, overage cap protection for first 90 days, and an option to increase commit at fixed uplift after month 6. Finance wants clearer ARR waterfall and mitigation if usage is undercommit.

Technical notes / requirements:
- Must support SSO (SAML) for developer console access.
- KMS integration mandatory for key wrapping; audit logs retention 1 year.
- Data residency: EU tenant for analytics dataset, routing by region required.
- Fallback policy desired: route to smaller quantized variant if primary model capacity saturated.

Commercial points captured (open):
- Commit sizing: customer asks to set initial commit equivalent to 300k tokens/day but billable ramp months expected.
- Burst: customer requests 2.5x burst for 7-day product launches, Redwood proposed 2x for 90 days trial then review.
- Overage: they want overage softened (cap at +25% of monthly invoice for first 6 months). Legal flagged retention/residency language.

Next steps (short):
- Marco to send updated term sheet by 2026-03-15 with revised ramp and burst language.
- Priya to validate VPC peering runbook and produce KMS integration notes by 2026-03-17.
- Legal to exchange data residency clause redlines (drive link).

Sales cues and value drivers:
- Emphasize predictable unit economics and burst safety net for product launches.
- Highlight Optimize recommendations to lower token footprint (prefix caching, batching) to reduce commit needs.
- Offer a 3-month 'soft overage' buffer (account credits) as concession during onboarding.

Quotes / call snippets:
- CTO: "We can absorb some committed cost if we know bursts won't blow our budget on day 1."
- Head of Finance: "Need a clear ramp so we can forecast ARR into our revenue model."

source.notes:
Overview: Prospective critical-infrastructure operator (national grid SCADA integrator) evaluating Redwood Private for air-gapped on-prem inference. Needs turnkey appliance + private control plane offline.

Timeline:
- 2025-11-14: Intro call (AE: Ethan) — initial requirements capture.
- 2025-12-08: Security deep-dive with infra/security (SE: Maya) — asked for HSM/FIPS details.
- 2026-01-25: On-site demo (air-gapped POC) — initial POC succeeded for basic chat + embeddings offline.
- 2026-02-18: Procurement submitted draft SOW; legal asked for indemnity language; awaiting vendor HSM cert.
- 2026-03-02: Tech review with ops — asked for deterministic fallback to quantized local models, model pinning, and offline update process.

Requirements summary:
- Workloads: operator chat, runbook search, telemetry reranking, post-incident triage.
- Latency: 99p < 200ms for short operator queries; burst batching for retro analysis (500 qps across cluster).
- Security: no external egress of tokens, mandatory HSM (FIPS 140-2), SAML SSO, audit logs retained 7 years, UK data residency.
- Deployment: fully air-gapped on-prem appliance; manual model binary ingestion; offline metrics export via encrypted removable media.

Quotes / risk:
- "Cannot accept any external egress for model tokens or telemetry." — Head of Ops.
- Concern: procurement lead time for CLINs; HSM vendor needs pre-approval.

POC notes:
- POC ran in isolated rack Nov-Jan. Basic chat + embedding workflows passed functional tests.
- Failure modes: KV-cache restore after power loss needs improvement; SE recommended snapshot + WAL approach.

Next actions:
- Provide FIPS/HSM vendor integration matrix and SOW template (due 2026-03-17).
- Legal & procurement want updated SLA for air-gapped installs; awaiting Redwood legal.

Links:
- Fireflies: ff-2026-03-02-oxf-demo
- Gmail threads: thread-oxf-procurement-2026-02
- Drive: drive:/shared/oxf-sentinel/security-deck-oxf-2026.pdf
- Jira: INF-4521 (environment provisioning), SEC-3179 (HSM cert questions)

AE notes: prioritize private-on-prem packaging, propose dedicated install engineering team and 2-week on-site commissioning window. Budget comfortable if SLAs/cert checks clear.

source.requirements_summary: Mid-market product analytics company building a user-facing chat assistant and semantic product search. Primary goals: predictable per-month inference cost, p95 latency <150ms for NA and <200ms for EU, throughput ~800-1,200 requests/sec for peak flows. source.notes:
CRM notes (shorthand, quotes, and fragments):
- "POC went well; we like latency profile but need price predictability" — PM (Ravi).
- POC scope: chat UI + search rerank path + periodic embedding syncs. Used redwood-hosted for speed; running comparisons vs current cloud infra.
- Pricing negotiation focus: committed monthly token/GPU equivalent vs burst credits. Pelmont's finance prefers lower upfront commit and a steeper ramp (month 1 = 25% commit, month 2 = 50%, month 3 = 75%, then 100%). Redwood standard proposal: 12-mo term, commit = 150M tokens/mo (or equivalent reserved GPU hours), burst up to 2.5x for 30 min sustained, overage charged at 60% of list hosted rate; option for true-up quarterly.
- AE note: propose alternative 9-mo ramp schedule with trial credit for months 1-2 to ease finance concerns. SE suggests token-capping per-route and hard throttle fallback routing to hosted API to avoid unexpected overages.
- Legal asks: SOC2 Type II copy, ISO certs, KMS integration spec, and explicit EU residency SOW clause for dataplane and logs retention. IL: "we cannot accept logs leaving EU", product compliance lead says.
- Technical: target p95 NA = 130ms on Dedicated (8xA100 equiv pool), observed hosted p95 = 180ms for same workload during POC under typical traffic. Pelmont wants SLA for latency once on Dedicated.
- Commercial sticky points: billing cadence (monthly vs quarterly), up-front discount for annual prepay, and clear definition of 'burst' (duration, throttles).
- Next: build SOW draft (commercial + residency + SLAs) and circulate to Pelmont procurement; finalize commit banding and overage formula.
- Internal tips: flag for sales leadership — likely close if legal & finance concessions on ramp/credits are accepted.
- Quick links: POC logs and perf traces stored in drive:/poc/pelmont/; Fireflies ids attached above.
- Quote from CTO (Slack): "If we can hit 130ms p95 w/ predictable ops spend, we can switch to Dedicated by Q2 close."
source.unique_highlights: Pricing/packaging negotiation centered on graded ramp schedule (25/50/75/100), burst credit semantics (2.5x for short sustained windows), and overage discount (60% of hosted list). Careful SOW language for EU data residency and audit logs required. Prefers VPC Dedicated but wants fallback chain to hosted API for capacity events.
source.sales_playbook_notes: Recommend 1) offer 2-month trial credit on Dedicated commit to reduce initial commit friction, 2) include a 'burst buffer' clause with clear duration & throttling, 3) provide SOC2 doc + KMS spec in one package, 4) propose monthly true-up with cap to avoid sticker shock, 5) include SLA for p95 latency once committed capacity is in prod.
source.pricing_proposal_history:
v1 (2026-03-02): 12-mo commit, 150M tokens/mo, burst 2.5x, overage 60% list, annual prepay discount 12%
v1.1 (2026-03-09): adjusted ramp option added — 25/50/75/100 over 4 months; 9-mo alternate shared; trial credits for months 1-2 pending approval
source.decision_criteria: Cost predictability (primary), latency SLOs, security/compliance (SOC2+residency), ability to do gradual ramp with clear overage rules, operational observability (per-route token breakdown).

source.notes:
CRM notes:
- Company: Ironbridge Medical AI (large regional health system running AI for clinician workflow).
- Primary pain: strict regulatory + residency requirements across US & EU, must be able to run inference in customer VPC.
- Key ask: Private VPC deployment with SSO (SAML/Okta), SCIM provisioning for automated user/groups, and fine-grained RBAC exposed in Redwood Console/control plane mapped to AD groups.
- Security stack: customer uses AWS (GovCloud for some workloads) + Splunk for SIEM. They require: customer-managed KMS keys (CMK), audit log streaming to Splunk/CloudWatch, 7-year retention for logs, and SOC2/ISO27001 evidence.
- Quote from CISO: "No console admin user that isn't bound to our AD groups — full RBAC and SCIM mapping is non-negotiable."
- Technical notes from SE call: need SAML metadata template, SCIM provisioning test plan, sample mapping for role -> AD group -> Redwood role. Customer expects SCIM to provision service accounts for clinicians (limited privileges) and AD groups for admins. Also asked about offboarding automation and SCIM delta sync cadence.
- KMS: wants BYOK via AWS KMS CMK in their account; asked about key rotation policy and encryption-at-rest proof.
- Audit logs: must include token-level telemetry, per-request headers (redacted PII), and be exportable in JSONL to their S3 + forwarded to Splunk. Retention policy: 7 years for audit logs; 30 days for raw request content (customer will set retention_controls).
- Compliance: procurement needs SOC2 + ISO27001 artifacts; requested Red Team summary and penetration test cadence.
- POC: 4-week private VPC POC requested (baseline latency/throughput for clinical summarization pipelines, certifying RBAC + SCIM flows). POC success criteria: SAML SSO working with Okta, SCIM provisioning creating/removing users and groups, RBAC enforcement in console, KMS encrypt/decrypt with CMK in customer account, audit logs delivered to S3 and visible in Splunk.
- Risk notes: data residency requirement for EU patient data - need region pinning + data flow diagram for legal.
- Action items: (1) Send SAML metadata + quickstart (DONE 2026-03-10). (2) Share SCIM sample attribute mapping by 2026-03-12. (3) Draft KMS BYOK integration note and responsibilities doc. (4) Schedule InfoSec final review (target 2026-03-20).
- Short-hand items: procurement lead = Maria Velasquez; "legal wants DPA + SLAs updated"; internal champion = Dr. Simon Hartman (VP Clinical AI).
- Misc links: drive:/deals/ironbridge/IB_Security_FAQ_v2.pdf, ff_2026_02_18_1923 transcript, INF-7421 (SE asked about kernel quantization compatibility).

STATUS: moving to procurement. Mid-market SaaS (B2B) building an AI-driven in-app support assistant + enhanced product search. Tried Hosted API first; POC OK but unit cost at projected scale pushes them toward Dedicated.

Key quotes
- "We want predictable monthly spend — spikes ok but need clear overage policy." — Head of Platform
- "Latency target: 150ms p95 for chat front-end, embeddings retrieval < 50ms for reranking."

POC findings (hosted):
- Re-ranking using 4k-context embeddings + knn performed well functionally; token costs will blow past $X at 1M queries/mo.
- Latency variability during peak hours ~200-400ms; not acceptable for live chat SLA.

Why Dedicated now:
- Need consistent p95 latency; desire for reserved GPU capacity and VPC connectivity for HIPAA-adjacent customers in their pipeline.
- Want clear ramp schedule: start with 2 reserved nodes (committed) month 1–3, add 4 nodes months 4–12 depending on adoption.

Pricing/Packaging issues on the table:
- Commit levels: customer prefers a 12-month commit but wants quarterly ramp (2 -> 6 nodes) and monthly reconciliation.
- Burst: must allow temporary burst capacity (up to 2x committed) at a known overage rate; they've pushed back on open-ended burst pricing.
- Overage: prefer flat per-GPU-hour overage vs. percentage uplift; want a cap on monthly overage charges (suggested by them: max 30% of committed spend).
- Ramp schedule: proposed by Redwood: tiered discounts as committed capacity grows (months 1–3 full price, months 4–6 10% discount, months 7–12 15%). Customer asked for steeper early discounts.

Open negotiation items:
- Final committed GPU-month count and calendar for ramp.
- Burst overage rate: customer asking for a defined $/GPU-hour number and a monthly cap.
- SLA specifics for on-prem-like availability (they require 99.9% for critical flows).

Next actions captured:
- Provide formal term sheet v3 with: explicit $/GPU-hour burst rate, overage cap, and proposed ramp discount schedule (Jordan to send by 2026-03-12).
- Legal to review our standard data residency clause and propose acceptable language for their counsel (Alex coordinating).
- Technical follow-up: capacity sizing workshop with customer infra team (Priya) — schedule wk of 2026-03-16.

Notes style: shorthand, bullets — internal. Refer to drive deck for line-item pricing.

Overage asked as flat $/GPU-hour, proposed cap 30% of commit. Burst allowed up to 2x with pre-agreed rate. Ramp schedule negotiation ongoing.

source.notes:
Summary: CounselMesh is a mid-size e-discovery and compliance provider moving from a homegrown Elastic+vector plugin to a vendor-managed RAG stack. They are heavy on retention/audit requirements and want a private/VPC deployment with customer-managed keys.

Conversation highlights:
- "We need verifiable audit trails for every query and admin action; retention policies must be enforceable per-case." (Head of Security)
- High indexing scale: currently ~120M documents (mixed PDFs, native docs, audio transcripts). Estimated growth to 250M over 18 months.
- Expected query profile: 30-50k embedding lookups/day, peak concurrent rerank pipelines ~600 qps. Overall latency SLO: <= 200ms for 1-to-5 candidate rerank path for top-5 results.
- Cost sensitivity: prefer cost-per-query model for embeddings+rerank, want tooling to project unit costs for different batching/quantization profiles.

What they liked about Redwood:
- Unified private control plane option + clear audit log expectations.
- Optimize recommendations (batching/quantization) to reduce cost without sacrificing recall.

Open items / asks:
- Security questionnaire (SaaS + private) answered and returned by 2026-03-15.
- Demo environment in customer VPC or private pilot with sample index (50k docs) for performance validation.
- Detailed cost model: embeddings storage + ANN index ops + reranking compute per 1M queries.

AE notes (Maya):
- Pricing will likely be a 12-month committed Dedicated+Optimize bundle. Push to close by May 2026.
- Legal already signaled strict data residency clauses; need legal ops to pre-approve our MSA redlines.

Staged POC plan (high level):
1) Security review & network onboarding (VPN cert exchange)
2) Pilot: 50k-doc index + baseline embeddings/rerank (2 weeks)
3) Scale test: simulate 600 qps rerank + 50k qps embedding lookups (expand as infra allows)
4) Cost/quality evaluation & contract signature.

source.notes:
Account summary: Solstice is a mid-to-large CX platform (SaaS) serving telco and retail enterprise customers. They signed with our hosted API for chat features last year and are now evaluating a major expansion to run their contact-center agent-assist and ticket automation workloads on Dedicated/Private.

Recent timeline and activities:
- 2026-02-05: Discovery call (Leah + Ravi) — product fit for agent-assist: intent extraction, tool-calling to Salesforce and back-end orchestration. Customer emphasized need for deterministic tool-calls and audit trail.
- 2026-02-12: Technical deep-dive (Ravi) — discussed KV cache-pin for session affinity, autoscaling during peak spikes, and quantization tradeoffs. Fireflies call id ff-20260212-4419.
- 2026-02-20: Security RFI submitted; attached security FAQ in Drive. Asking about KMS/HSM integration and data residency in EU.
- 2026-03-01: POC kickoff — 2-week load test plan to validate 8k concurrent agents (avg 1 req/sec per agent during spikes). Demo recording + transcript at ff-20260301-8732.
- 2026-03-08: Mid-POC sync — observed token throughput variance on long context (>8k tokens). SE suggested model routing to 7B quantized variants for cost/latency.
- 2026-03-09: Business sponsor (Head of Support Ops) confirmed intent to expand to EU contact centers Q3; asked for formal pricing and SLA ladder. Gmail thread thread-f:1618ef12 contains sponsor email.

Use-cases & requirements (customer language/fragments):
- "Agent assist must return suggested replies + 1-click tool-call to CRM/OMS"
- Ticket summarization: 1-2 sentence TL;DR + key action items and confidence score.
- Reranking with embeddings for knowledge base retrieval (sub-200ms median for short responses).
- Reliability during black-friday-style spikes: scale to 8k concurrent agents with <3% error budget.
- Function/tool-calling safety: deterministic retries, idempotency, and full audit trail.

Technical preferences and constraints:
- Prefer open/quantized model family (Llama/Mistral family) for on-prem/dedicated; fallback to smaller distill models for cold starts.
- VPC/private deployment required for EU node; data residency and logs retention controls are gating.
- SSO/SAML + SCIM for user provisioning; SOC2 + ISO27001 are must-haves for security team.
- Latency targets: <200ms median for agent suggestions, p95 <800ms during normal load; for spikes want graceful degradation with cached suggestions.

Commercial/ops notes:
- Existing hosted spend ~$120k ARR; expansion expected to move to committed Dedicated contract. Estimated ARR $1M-$3M depending on committed concurrency and retention.
- Primary blocker: legal/DTSA clause around data residency and export controls for EU customers.
- Pricing ask: prefer committed RU + overage model, need explicit per-token pricing for quantized variants and dedicated GPU types.

Next steps (short):
- Leah to submit MSA draft to Solstice legal (WIP) — target close 2026-04.
- SE to run extended throughput bench (RDW-6312) across p3-equivalent hardware; provide results by 2026-03-20.
- CSM to prepare onboarding plan for EU ops and SAML integration checklist.

Quick wins / talking points for renewal pitch:
- Redwood automatic prefix caching reduces token spend for repetitive agent prompts — potential 25-40% cost saving.
- Dedicated pools + failover to smaller quantized model variants keeps UX consistent during spikes.
- Console dashboards can map token cost to agent/team for chargeback (requested by Finance).

Attachments / refs: pricing deck, security FAQ (Drive), POC test plan (attached to Gmail thread). Jira ticket RDW-6312 tracking throughput bench, support ticket SUP-4027 for initial onboarding issue.

source.notes:
Overview: Lakedale Shielded Compute is a regional critical infrastructure operator evaluating a fully air-gapped on-prem Redwood Private deployment. Primary goal: enable a secure chat assistant and classified-doc summarization pipeline for operations centers with strict non-egress rules.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-02-20: Intro call (AE Maya Chen) — high-level requirements captured; site classification = IL3; customer emphasized no internet egress.
- 2026-02-25: Technical deep-dive (SE Jonah Blake) — reviewed network segmentation, allowed OS images (RHEL8/Rocky8), signed package requirements, and HSM/KMS constraints.
- 2026-03-01: Security workshop (security + compliance triage) — Fireflies ff-20260301-09a7b3; covered FIPS, STIG-like baseline, and allowed telemetry.
- 2026-03-04: Onsite validation window requested for 2026-03-22; must include performance run with preloaded models, no internet, and host-based attestations.

Technical / requirements summary: 
- Workloads: low-latency chat for incident triage (concurrent operators ~120, expected burst to 300), secure batch summarization and embeddings for closed archives (daily batch ~10k documents).
- Latency/throughput targets: 95th pct token latency <= 150ms for short-chat flows; embeddings throughput ~200 qps for batch indexing windows.
- Models: preference for open LLMs with Redwood-verified quantized variants. Customer requires model binaries to be delivered as signed artifacts and stored on internal object store.
- Hardware: procurement window expects H100-class nodes (32–80GB) or equivalent; infra team requires guidance on GPU count for target SLOs.
- Routing/fallback: must support in-cluster fallback to smaller quantized variant (no external model fallback allowed).

Security / compliance specifics: 
- Must run in fully air-gapped mode; no outbound telemetry/call-home unless explicit signed addendum.
- KMS: integrate with on-prem HSM (PKCS#11) and FIPS-validated key lifecycle.
- Audit: detailed per-request audit logs retained for 7+ years (exportable to internal SIEM).
- Controls: SSO via SAML 2.0, RBAC mapping to internal groups, SLA for timely security patches and signed release artifacts.
- Hardening: STIG/SCAP scans required before acceptance; container images must be reproducible and signed.

POC plan / milestones: 
- Deliver signed DPA and air-gap appendix.
- Provide offline installation media and signed model artifacts.
- Week-of 2026-03-22: On-site baseline perf run and integration with HSM; run Redwood benchmark suite against their representative prompt sets.
- Security sign-off expected after STIG pass and offline acceptance testing.

Quotes / paraphrases from stakeholders: 
- CISO: "No staged internet egress — everything must be inspectable and signable."
- Infra lead: "We need a repeatable air-gap install and a playbook for model updates that doesn't rely on outbound access."

Next steps (explicit):
- Legal: finalize DPA + telemetry addendum (owner: Maya).
- Security: confirm FIPS attestation timeline for customer HSM (owner: Priya).
- SE: prepare discrete runbook and offline media; include model-sizing guidance for target SLOs (owner: Jonah).

Open questions / blockers (short):
- Can Redwood provide a documented offline model-update workflow with signed manifests?
- Confirmation of exact HSM vendor and firmware for FIPS compatibility.
- Clarify acceptable retention window and export format for audit logs.

Linked artifacts:
- Fireflies meeting id: ff-20260301-09a7b3
- Gmail thread: thread-20260228-IX7YQ
- Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/secure-procurement-Lakedale-2026
- Jira: SEC-9123 (security scoping), INFRA-4482 (hardware sizing)

CRM style brief:
- Sales play: enterprise procurement — heavy legal + security track.
- Tone: conservative, slow cadence, decision driven by security board.
- Win criteria: air-gap install verified, STIG/SCAP pass, DPA signed, baseline perf meets SLOs.

Quotes: VP Product - "Need predictable unit economics; can't have wild monthly swings." CTO - "We'll accept controlled burst (2x) for short product launches; need price for that." Pricing sensitivity: target blended cost below $0.20 per 1k tokens for chat + embeddings budgeted. AE notes: AE presented Dedicated sizing options (4-GPU baseline / 8-GPU scale plan) and proposed a 3-month ramp (50% commit month1, 75% month2, 100% month3) with 72-hr burst allowance and stepped overage bands. Procurement pushed back asking for annual commit discount + carry-forward credits. Next demo scheduled to walk legal through term sheet and burst clauses. source.notes:
Account snapshot: Solaris Model Ops is an ecommerce personalization platform looking to move model serving for ranking + session-level chat to Redwood Dedicated. Heavy seasonality (Q4 peaks) — needs strict throughput guarantees and well-defined burst policies.

Short summary of needs and constraints:
- Primary workload: real-time reranking + candidate scoring (reranking) with occasional contextual chat for shopper assistance.
- Target p95 token latency: <= 120ms for text-generation endpoints (single-turn recommendation explanations).
- Minimum sustained throughput (negotiation): 25,000 tokens/sec across the dedicated cluster during normal peak; allow bursts up to 60,000 tokens/sec for up to 15 minutes during flash sales.
- Concurrency caps requested: 200 concurrent active sessions for ranking endpoints; 50 concurrent for long-chat sessions. Prefer per-route concurrency limits.
- Reserved GPU ask: initial commit: 8 x A100-80GB equivalent or 6 x H100 (Redwood to advise mix). Want headroom for 30% growth in peak capacity.
- SLA asks: 99.9% uptime for dedicated service; throughput guarantee clause: minimum tokens/sec with credit/penalty schedule if below threshold for >5 consecutive minutes.
- Burst policy: Solaris wants automatic burst above reserved baseline (2x sustained) for short intervals, with configurable throttling and predictable overage pricing. Legal wants hard caps on unbounded burst.
- Fallback behavior: require automated routing to hosted fallback model tier on capacity shortage; preserve per-request data residency and audit logs.
- Security: enterprise SSO, encryption at rest, KMS integration for model artifacts, 13-month audit log retention, SOC2 required.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-01-15: Lead created by growth; initial discovery call with Jordan B.
- 2026-01-27: Technical scoping workshop with Maya (SE) and infra team — collected baseline qps/latency metrics and traffic patterns (daily and seasonal windows).
- 2026-02-12: Deep-dive on throughput guarantees (Fireflies ff-2026-02-12-9041). Solaris asked for explicit min tokens/sec and burst definition.
- 2026-02-25: POC kickoff — synthetic load harness + 30k sustained tokens/sec target; Redwood to deliver cluster config and perf run plan.
- 2026-03-02: First POC perf run completed (internal). Observed sustained 28k tokens/sec with 6x A100 equiv; p95 latency ~140ms on gen tasks; note: KV-cache hit rate affected by session churn.
- 2026-03-05: Follow-up meeting — Solaris rejects p95 latency of 140ms (target 120ms). SE proposed kernel tuning + CPU offload and higher GPU count.
- 2026-03-08: Redwood proposed two configurations: (A) 8 x A100 + aggressive batching, (B) 6 x H100 + lower batching; both include automatic prefix cache and 10-min burst window.
- 2026-03-09: Pricing/contract sync: Solaris legal flagged bandwidth/overage clause and wants predefined credit schedule for throughput breaches.

POC artifacts / test plan notes:
- Load harness: JMeter + custom token-level generator (drive:/deals/solarismodelops/poc-harness/).
- Metrics collected: tokens/sec, per-request token latency, GPU utilization, KV cache hit rate, queue depth.
- Acceptance: sustained >=25k tokens/sec for 1 hour at p95 <=120ms; <1% error rate.

Negotiation specifics / suggested Redwood stance (internal):
- Offer a two-tier throughput SLA: baseline (guaranteed) = 25k tokens/sec with credits if below for >30m aggregated per day; burst window up to 60k tokens/sec for 15 minutes with soft throttling and per-minute metering.
- Concurrency: enforce per-route caps (200 / 50) with clear failure mode (429 with Retry-After) and optional queueing for warm calls.
- Reserved GPU sizing: recommend H100-heavy for Redistributed p95 targets; propose initial 6 H100 with option to scale to 8 within 30 days if POC shows TPU-like bottlenecks. Include right-sizing clause after 90-day telemetry review.
- Pricing: include burst credit pool (prepaid tokens for bursts) and overage rates tiered by 2x baseline, 3x baseline.
- Legal / SLA wording: define measurement window (1-min sampling, 5-min rolling avg), credit calculation, and mitigation plan (auto-fallback to hosted models + notification).

Direct quotes from stakeholders:
- "We can't accept >5% degradation during our flash sale windows — even short outages cost millions." — Solaris Head of Product.
- "Need deterministic behavior more than raw peak numbers; predictable throttles are better than silent slowdowns." — Lead SRE.

Open action items (short):
- SE (Maya) to run second optimized POC with H100 config and improved KV cache strategy (target: p95<=120ms). Due 2026-03-17.
- AE (Jordan) to pull draft SLA with two-tier throughput guarantee and sample credit schedule; deliver to Solaris legal by 2026-03-12 (delayed).
- Product to provide documented burst policy options and exact throttle behavior for inclusion in MSA.
- Finance to finalize estimated ARR band and draft prepay burst credit pricing.

Risk notes:
- If POC fails to reach p95 target with reasonable GPU count, Solaris will explore in-house optimized serving or competitor H100 offerings.
- Legal friction on credit calculation could delay close by 4+ weeks.

Stubs / refs:
- Drive proposal link: drive:/deals/solarismodelops/proposal-throughput-2026-02.pdf
- Fireflies: ff-2026-02-12-9041
- Jira escalation for POC infra tuning: INF-1127

source.notes:
Summary: Mid-market CX SaaS evaluating Redwood Hosted API for quick rollout, then moving to Dedicated for production. Team: Product lead (Cara V.), Head of Infrastructure (J. Morales), CTO (A. Singh). We completed hosted PoC (chat + embeddings) in Jan; performance good, cost per 1k tokens ~ competitive.

POC outcomes:
- Hosted API used for 4-week pilot: 95th pct latency ~140ms for short chat flows, embeddings throughput met batch targets.
- Issues: token-usage growth larger than estimated due to multi-turn customer context caching, led to cost variance.
- Decision: prefer Dedicated to control unit economics + VPC isolation for PII-sensitive logs.

Pricing/packaging discussion (current):
- Ridgecrest requests 12-month Dedicated commit. Suggested commit tiers being negotiated: 300M, 600M, and custom 900M token bundles. AE proposed 600M commit with monthly ramp and 25% burst pool on top.
- Key ask: predictable overage terms (flat per-token overage vs tiered) — finance prefers a capped overage at a negotiated rate or auto-burst with soft cap.
- Ramp: customer wants a three-month ramp (30% / 60% / 100%) tied to feature launch milestones. They want the option to increase commit without penalty if forecast changes.
- Burst: require guaranteed burst for daily peak events (up to 2x baseline) and ability to consume additional burst credits for a short fee.

Security & compliance:
- Must support SSO (SAML) + SCIM, SOC2 compliance proof, customer-managed KMS for key rotation, audit log retention for 3+ years, and US data residency.
- Legal: awaiting answers on incident RTO/RPO, breach notification timelines. Security team requested security FAQ and network connectivity diagram (provided).

Procurement notes:
- Pricing deck reviewed 2026-02-18 (see drive link). Finance flagged ARR threshold for enterprise discounts. Procurement expects final T&Cs and SOW appendix with commit/burst/overage table.
- Stakeholders: CTO (A. Singh) sign-off, Head of Ops (J. Morales) technical sign-off, VP Product (C. Vora) budget owner.

Dialogue excerpts (paraphrase):
- "We like the Hosted PoC results; predictable cost is the blocker for GA." — VP Product.
- "Need a burst model that doesn't punish us for seasonal spikes." — Head of Infrastructure.

Open actions:
- Redwood price team to propose 2 committed options: 600M + 25% burst vs 900M with lower per-token price and 15% burst. (Assigned: Maya)
- SE to validate KV cache tuning for Ridgecrest flows to reduce token usage (Assigned: Ethan).
- Legal to circulate draft SOW with explicit ramp schedule and overage cap (Assigned: Legal).

Notes style: shorthand, action-item focused. Keep follow-up cadence weekly until contract signed.

source.capacity_requirements_summary: Hosted trial (3 weeks) consumed ~1.2M tokens; observed peak QPS 14 (sustained 6-8), burst up to 30. Trial latency target 300ms; observed 180-450ms depending on prompt length. Production forecast: sustained QPS 50-60, peak bursts 120 for seasonal spikes; target concurrency 20-30. Token mix: ~70% short prompts (<=150 tokens), ~20% mid (150-800), ~10% long context (>=2000 tokens) used for multi-doc synthesis. Embeddings: ~300k calls/month (avg 512-d vectors), reranking calls ~120k/month. Recommended Dedicated pool: 3-4 GPUs (A40/H100-equivalent) with autoscale for burst; KV/prefix cache needed to keep tail-latency low.
source.notes:
Quick summary / CRM-style notes:
- Company: Silverdune builds an in-app product insights assistant for B2B SaaS customers (trial users -> paid). Mid-market customer base; fairly price sensitive but values reliability.
- Started with Redwood Hosted API to validate model choices and latency profile. Trial ran 3 weeks; dev team integrated widget into staging.
- Trial metrics: ~1.2M tokens total, avg token size ~120, peak QPS 14, median latency 220ms. Observed heavy tail on long-context documents (10% of calls but ~40% of token volume).
- Primary ask now: sizing & pricing for Dedicated reserved capacity. They want predictable per-request latency and to avoid noisy-neighbor surprises during feature launches.
- Security: require SSO (SAML), KMS for key management, audit logs with 90-day retention, and option for EU residency for some customers. SOC2 is required; they asked whether Redwood has a customer-facing SSAE/SOC2 report (SE provided link).
- Cost sensitivity: product lead said "need to keep inference costs below $0.02/1k tokens on average for our long-term plan" — negotiating with FinOps. Wants a committed ARR with progressive tiers.
- Technical constraints: May infra freeze (internal) — SSO rollout target mid-May; Dedicated cutover needs to be before feature launch in July.
- POC ask: 2-week dedicated POC (discounted) to validate tail-latency under 10k concurrent users in staging. Wants telemetry hooks (trace ids) into Redwood Console.
- Legal: DPA + standard security addendum, racked against their procurement timeline (PO by 2026-04).
- SE notes: consider auto-burst to hosted for overflow, prefix caching turned on for product Q&A to reduce tokens. Suggested quantized model variant for cost savings on non-critical paths.
- Next steps assigned: AE (Lena) to deliver dedicated quote + tiered AR commitments; SE (Rafael) to produce GPU sizing worksheet and run a short smoke test with customer dataset; Legal loop to start on DPA.
- Quote expectation: commit to 12 months, estimate ARR ~100k-250k depending on burst allowances and extra observability retention.
source.technical_observations:
Performance: good median latencies under hosted trial; tail caused by repeated long-context regen. Caching effective for repeated prompts (reduced tokens by ~18% for FAQ flows). Embeddings latency stable (~80ms p50).
Billing: trial overage calculations surprised product finance — need clear dedicated pricing for token mixes and embeddings vs. generation.
Operational: customer wants per-route dashboards, token-level breakouts, and alerting on quality regressions (ties to Redwood Optimize).
source.procurement_notes:
Customer prefers a single vendor agreement (Redwood) with optional seat-adds. Wants line-item for Dedicated reserved capacity and separate line for extra observability retention. Finance asks for 60-day invoice terms.
AE to push final pricing by 2026-03-17 for procurement review.

Account background: enterprise adtech platform ingesting live bidding signals + user profiles. First contact: 2026-01-12 (intro demo). Current ask: predictable throughput for production chat and reranking pipelines with multi-region failover.

Procurement summary (spring 2026):
- They want Dedicated GPU pools reserved across NA and EU to support GDPR traffic residency and low-latency bidder recency.
- Primary requirement: 99.95% availability SLA for dedicated pools + transparent latency SLOs (p50/p95/p99) by route.
- Traffic profile: peak concurrent inference streams ~4k QPS aggregate (chat + rerank), steady-state 1.2k QPS. Burst factor: up to 3x during campaign launches.
- Latency targets: chat p95 < 120ms token-gen on 13B-quantized models; rerank end-to-end p95 < 80ms for 1k-item rerank at 3x batch optimization.
- Throughput guarantees requested: sustained 1.5k QPS per region with 30% failover headroom (capacity planning ask: reserve 2k QPS equivalent GPUs per primary region).
- AZ topology: want 3 AZs per region, active-active routing. Failover policy: graceful degrade to quantized smaller model family (fallback) when capacity hit 85%.

Technical meetings / timeline:
- 2026-02-18: Deep-dive with SE (Priya). Reviewed model families (mix of open 13B/70B variants); agreed to benchmark 13B quantized as baseline. (FF-2026-02-18-0910)
- 2026-03-02: Architecture review with infra and networking teams. Discussed VPC peering, cross-region routing, KV cache priming, and eviction policies. Action: provide pool-sizing spreadsheet and warmup plan. (FF-2026-03-02-1134)
- 2026-03-08: Legal + procurement call to surface SLA language and refund/credit mechanics. They pushed back on termination clauses for sustained SLA breaches.

POC / technical asks for dedicated evaluation:
- 2-week soak (prod-like) in NA primary region: dedicated pool sized to 60% of forecasted peak to validate autoscaling and KPI stability.
- Benchmarks: p50/p95/p99 token latency per model; throughput sustained tests at 1k/1.5k/3k QPS; KV cache hit-rate targets >75% for prefix-heavy traffic.
- Multi-region failover test: simulated NA region outage and measure failover latency delta and cold-KV impact. Expected warmup runway < 45s for critical routes.
- Observability: need token-level cost/latency breakdown, per-model dashboards, per-route tracing hooks, and audit logs pushed to their SIEM.

Commercial / SLA negotiation points:
- Pricing: they prefer an annual reserved commit with monthly invoicing; want option for quarterly scaling adjustments with a 10% capacity change penalty.
- SLA: 99.95% pool availability, with credits tiered and cap on cumulative credits. They asked for a higher remedy (partial termination) if repeated breaches across three months—legal flagged.
- Commitment: targeting 9–12 month initial reserved term for Dedicated; optional add-on to run Redwood Private in EU for data residency later in the year.

Open risks and internal notes (AE/SE):
- Capacity math: conservative sizing recommended — allocate 30% headroom per-region and 15% cross-region failover buffer. This maps to 2x baseline GPU count for tactical spikes.
- Cost sensitivity: medium; they trade some unit cost for guaranteed latency/throughput. Optimize team to propose quantization + batching profile to reduce committed GPU footprint.
- Security: SOC2 + SSO baseline ok; need to provide encryption-at-rest KMS flow and audit log export sample for procurement.

Next steps (short):
- Deliver detailed multi-region capacity diagram and GPU count spreadsheet (AE + SE). Due 2026-03-12.
- Confirm contract SLA language options for credits vs termination; legal to propose alternative remedies by 2026-03-18.
- Schedule 2-week soak POC for NA region (target start 2026-03-22) and coordinate with their infra calendar.

Quotes / snippets from calls:
- "If we don't have a solid failover plan, campaign launches will cost us millions in lost impressions." — Head of Infra
- "We need to see telemetry at token level to reconcile cost to business KPIs." — VP Product

Stubs / artifacts referenced:
- capacity spreadsheet: drive:/deals/silverpine/capacity-sizing-v1.xlsx
- pricing deck: drive:/deals/silverpine/pricing-deck-v3.pdf
- security FAQ: https://drive.google.com/open?id=sec-faq-silverpine

AE notes (Evan): keep negotiation flexible on credit mechanics; push for technical acceptance criteria in SOW to limit termination risk. SE (Priya) to validate KV cache warmup pattern and provide recommended batching profile before POC.

requirements_summary: Workloads: conversational chat (customer support, bot), real-time reranking for candidate search, periodic embeddings refresh
Latency targets: chat p50 <= 30ms, p95 <= 120ms, p99 budgeting for 250ms; reranking p95 <= 200ms
Throughput targets: baseline sustained 600 QPS for chat during business hours, peak bursts to 1200 QPS for short windows
Embeddings: 800-2k vectors/sec depending on bulk jobs; can be scheduled for off-peak but need bounded completion time
Availability: multi-region failover with RTO goal <5 minutes for traffic reroute; prefer active-primary + warm-standby secondary
Fault tolerance: N+1 reserved GPU headroom per region; 20-25% spare capacity planned for autoscaling and failover
Routing: policy-based by geo + latency SLO; automatic fallback to lighter/quantized model when primary capacity saturated
Data controls: no cross-region token logging for EU-subject data; audit logs retained 1 year with exportable deliveries to customer S3/KMS
technical_summary: Requested architecture: Dedicated GPU pools in us-east-1 and us-west-2, AZ-spread across 2 AZs each region
Sizing ask: initial reserved pool = 12 H100 equiv (balanced option) with burst pool (shared, billed) for seasonal spikes
Failover headroom: maintain minimum 20% spare capacity in secondary region (warm-standby) to absorb primary failure
Autoscale policy: scaling step size 2 GPUs, scale cooldown 90s, pre-warm policy to keep prefix cache hot for chat flows
Model variants: primary = fp16 70B Llama-derivative; fallback = 8-bit quantized compatible variant with slightly higher latency but lower cost
Throughput guarantees: target throughput SLAs per model-route (tokens/sec & concurrent requests), to be formalized in SLA appendix
Observability: token-level cost dashboard, per-route p50/p95/p99, KV cache hit rate, cold-start count; require daily retention summary
POC plan: synthetic replay of 72h production trace at 1:1 QPS for 2h, measure p95/p99, tail latency under degradation, and failover time
sla_priorities: 99.95% availability for dedicated pool (regional) with defined credit schedule
Latency SLOs by route/model (p95 targets outlined above) with exemption for scheduled maintenance
Defined MTTR for region outage; documented runbook for cross-region cutover and rollback
Clear definitions for maintenance windows and pre-notification windows (>=72 hours for non-emergency)
po_details: Customer prefers yearly reserved contract with quarterly review for usage adjustments
Asking for buffer clauses: ability to convert reserved GPUs between regions in 30-day notice
Request for firm pricing band for 12-18 month term; want no more than 5% annual price escalator
Billing: committed monthly invoice + burst usage billed monthly; needs reporting CSV with SKU-level consumption
meeting_notes: (3/08) Procurement: 'we need demonstrable SLA performance before we sign; credits must be meaningful.'
(2/28) SE: recommended N+1 in-region and 20% warm-standby cross-region — customer reacted positively to warm standby idea
(2/28) Action: SE to provide two concrete configurations: cost-optimized (8 H100 equiv) and resiliency-optimized (14 H100 equiv)
Customer asked for example failure scenarios: AZ-level outage, region-level outage, and GPU preemption. Wants clear runbook for each.

AE to circulate SLA draft v2 to procurement/legal (done), follow-up scheduled 2026-03-15
Infra team to confirm GPU SKU availability for Q2 (Ethan Park owning, ticket INF-4829)
Run POC synthetic load test 2026-03-18-19; SE and customer ops on call; FF recording planned

Confirm exact GPU SKUs & AZ distribution; produce deployment diagram for customer's security review
Agree on failover mechanics: traffic-steering TTLs, request-draining, prefix cache warm strategies
PO issuance after POC validates p95/p99 under expected traffic and failover exercises

source.notes:
Account: HighTide ML Platforms (platform for carrier routing + demand forecasting). Eng lead: CTO Maria Chen. Primary driver: predictable per-token cost and guaranteed latency for customer-facing chat and real-time reranking in routing UI. They have an internal SRE and a procurement timeline that pushes for signatures in Apr 2026.

Key points from discovery: 
- Want dedicated GPU pool (initial commit: 4x H100 equiv, scale to 12 within 3-6 months).
- Use cases: live chat for drivers (low-latency), reranking for route candidates (high throughput), nightly batch embedding compute for analytics.
- Strong requirement for VPC/private control plane + KMS integration. On-prem option for sensitive clients in EMEA.
- Cost sensitivity: wants clear unit economics per token / per-embedding and predictable monthly rate.

Quotes / call notes: 
- "We can't accept bursty cold-starts that kick users out of the UI" (Maria Chen).
- AE note: they requested a sample runbook for failover between regions.

SLA / legal interaction history: 
- Legal sent initial TS/PS SLA redlines 2026-02-28. 
- Redwood provided draft uptime table 2026-03-02. 
- Hightide countered with credit schedule 2026-03-05 (attached).

Other: pen-test required before go-live for any VPC/on-prem installs; schedule TBD.

Short sticky items: procurement wants 30-day invoice terms; security wants SOC2 plus explicit audit log retention 3 years.
source.sla_terms_summary:
HighTide requested SLA negotiation focused on uptime %, credit schedule, maintenance windows, support response times, and throughput guarantees. Proposed terms (Hightide asks):
- Uptime target: 99.95% for Dedicated pools (monthly measurement).
- Credit schedule requested by customer: 10% monthly credit for uptime <99.95% but >=99.0%, 25% for uptime <99.0% and >=98.0%, 50% credit for <98.0%.
- Redwood initial offer: 99.99% with stepped credits: 5% (<99.99% >=99.95), 15% (<99.95% >=99.0), 35% (<99.0%). Need to reach middle ground.
- Maintenance windows: prefer monthly 02:00-05:00 local (NA) scheduled; Hightide requires 48h advance notice for non-emergency maintenance and maximum 2 hours of user-impacting downtime per quarter.
- Support response / SLA tiers: P1 (system down/high impact): 15 min phone/Slack response, P2 (degraded performance): 1 hour, P3 (functional issue): 4 hours, P4: 24 hours. Customer also wants a named escalation engineer and monthly operational review for first 90 days.
- Throughput & latency SLOs: interactive chat p95 < 120ms, p99 < 350ms for short prompts (<=256 tokens) when routed to Dedicated pool; embeddings p95 < 70ms; reranking throughput guarantee: sustained 1,200 requests/min at 128-token average per pool as baseline. Redwood requires profiling to convert these into resource reservations (tokens/s per GPU equivalence).
- Reserved GPU planning: initial reservation 4x H100-class GPUs (or equivalent quantized A100s) with explicit scaling step to 8x at +20% usage and to 12x by end of Q3 if utilization thresholds met. Billing: committed monthly rate with overage at a pre-agreed burst rate.
- Failover & fallback: require documented fallback to lower-tier model (lower cost) with graceful degradation; legal wants rollback SLA and incident notification within 30 minutes for P1.

2026-03-05: Hightide returned counter-proposal to credit schedule

source.notes:
Background: Arkhaven runs a clinical triage + patient Q&A layer across several hospital networks. Currently on Redwood Hosted API with steady growth (1.2B tokens/mo). Primary drivers for expansion: predictable latency for emergent triage workflows, data residency for EMR-integrated sites, and unit-cost reduction for large-batch summarization pipelines.

source.notes: CRM notes - Cedarbridge (FS, EU HQ). Primary ask: Private VPC deployment with customer-managed KMS and strict data residency in eu-west-1. They run AML/risk-monitoring workloads (embeddings + reranking) where data cannot leave EU. Key items discussed: - SIG/CAIQ: Cedarbridge sent partial CAIQ. They expect completed CAIQ back plus clarifications on a handful of control responses (data deletion, third-party subprocessors). - Shared responsibility: they require explicit matrix stating Redwood handles runtime, OS patching, model weights, but customer controls keys, network, and ingress policies. Quotes sent - see drive deck.

source.notes:
Account snapshot: global market-data provider; latency-sensitive trading assist and real-time enrichment. Key asks centered on ironclad SLA (uptime + credits), deterministic latency, reserved GPU planning and predictable maintenance windows.

SLA negotiation highlights:
- Customer requests: 99.99% uptime for primary trading endpoints, 99.95% for non-critical APIs. Wants credits to scale and not be de minimis.
- Redwood baseline: proposed 99.95% platform SLA for Dedicated; offered add-on for 99.99% with increased reserved capacity and dedicated engineer.
- Credit schedule discussed (customer pushed for formula). Current proposed sliding scale (example):
  * uptime >= 99.95%: 0% credits
  * 99.9% <= uptime < 99.95%: 10% of monthly Dedicated fee
  * 99.0% <= uptime < 99.9%: 30% credit
  * uptime < 99.0%: 100% credit (customer insisted on cap at 100% monthly fee)
- Exclusions: scheduled maintenance, force majeure. Customer insists scheduled maintenance capped to 4 hours/month for trading endpoints and must have 72-hour advance notice.
- Maintenance windows: customer prefers 02:00-05:00 ET; Redwood recommended regional maintenance but agreed to white-list trading endpoints for patched lanes.

Latency & throughput SLOs:
- Latency targets for chat/trading assist: p95 < 120ms, p99 < 300ms on configured Dedicated cluster under expected concurrent sessions (10k active sessions).
- Throughput guarantees: baseline sustained throughput per cluster 8k tokens/sec (A100-equivalent); Keystone requests aggregate sustained 64k tokens/sec for production; Redwood proposed reserved pool of 8x A100-80GB nodes (baseline) + 2 hot-spare nodes, autoscale to 12 nodes for burst (30-min spin-up SLA).
- Fallbacks: automatic routing to lower-cost variants acceptable for non-critical queries, but trading endpoints must not fall back without explicit failover policy and customer consent.

Operational support / escalation:
- Support SLA: P1 (system down impacting trading) response < 15 minutes, P1 remediation updates every 30 minutes, 24/7 on-call; P2 < 60 minutes; P3 < 4 hours. Customer requests named on-call engineer for first 90 days post-go-live.
- Diagnostics: access to token-level latency traces, request logs (scrubbed PII), and billing breakdowns required for monthly review.

Security & compliance notes:
- CISO quote: "cannot accept key escrow — all keys must be customer-managed KMS; proof of key retention and rotation policy."
- Data residency: primary production in us-east-1, backups replicated to us-west-2; no eu data allowed for trading ML.

Commercials and GPU planning:
- Pricing sensitivity: willing to commit to 12-24 month reserved contract if SLA and maintenance windows acceptable. Estimated ARR band discussed $500k-$1.5M.
- GPU plan: initial reserved 8x A100 (80GB) per cluster; add 2 hot spares; option to burst to hosted pool (pay-as-you-go) for unpredictable spikes. Customer asked for clear lead time (4 weeks) for adding physical capacity in Dedicated.

Open legal redlines / blockers:
- Legal wants explicit language for scheduled maintenance caps and exclusion list; remove vague "reasonable efforts" language around credits.
- Need to clarify credit calculation baseline (is it regional availability zone or global cluster availability?).

Next steps (short):
- Redwood to provide SLA markups with explicit maintenance caps and sample credit clause by 2026-03-17.
- SE to deliver final GPU sizing quote and lead times; include hot-spare cost and burst rates.
- Legal to arrange redline session w/ Keystone legal (week of 2026-03-22).

Stubs / references: pricing deck and draft SLA in Drive (see linked_drive_docs). Fireflies IDs for conversation capture. Jira INFRA-1129 opened to capture provisioning lead-time question.
preferred_contract_terms: scheduled maintenance <= 4 hours/month for trading endpoints
credit tiers tied to monthly Dedicated fee
